description: Binary market on whether the Fed holds rates at the December 9, 2026 FOMC meeting. Currently pricing 67% hold, implying ~33% chance of at least one cut by year-end. Pair with April hold (95%) to see the implied rate path.
question: Will the Federal Reserve hold rates at their December 2026 meeting?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if the Federal Reserve makes no change to the federal funds rate (0 bps hike) at the December 9, 2026 FOMC meeting.

triggers.description: Federal Reserve FOMC statement announces no rate change

exclusions: Emergency rate changes between meetings do not affect this market
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Same cancellation edge case as April: if meeting is canceled, hold resolves Yes. This market prices cumulative rate path expectations — 67% hold vs April's 95% hold implies the market expects ~1-2 cuts between April and December 2026.

catalyst_dates.event: April FOMC decision — first data point in the rate path
June FOMC decision + updated dot plot and economic projections
September FOMC decision + updated dot plot
December FOMC decision + updated dot plot

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: No direct Polymarket equivalent for December hold/cut. Polymarket's closest is the Warsh/rate combo market (rate ≤ 2.5% by year-end), which bundles the rate path with Fed Chair confirmation — structurally different.

related_markets.notes: April 2026 hold at 95% — near-term anchor. Spread between April (95%) and December (67%) is the implied cut probability.
Rate above 3.50% after December at 63% — if holds continue through December, rate stays at current level (~4.25-4.50%)
